name: Senator Angus King
tweet: If you’d like to hear more about the Commission’s findings or process, we are about to launch our report at an event moderated by @IgnatiusPost. The event is being livestreamed, and you can watch here:  https://t.co/OIYWIHOI3G

name: Senator Angus King
tweet: One last note: our report is *completely unclassified* because improving cybersecurity can’t just happen behind closed doors in Washington. It requires work from each of us. I hope you’ll give it a read, and urge your elected officials to act on cyber before it’s too late.

name: Senator Angus King
tweet: These are just a few of our recommendations – and by no means is our list exhaustive. We concluded some debates without reaching a consensus, and in this ever-shifting sphere there will be new challenges to face that we can’t yet imagine. But this is a very good place to start.

name: Senator Angus King
tweet: ➡️We need the American people to be able to stay vigilant, and that starts with helping them understand foreign interference. The government should support digital literacy, civics education, and public awareness of malign foreign efforts.

name: Senator Angus King
tweet: ➡️The team at @CISAGov is doing important work already, and we need to elevate their efforts by giving the agency the authorities and funding it needs to reach its full potential.

name: Senator Angus King
tweet: ➡️Congress should establish a Senate-confirmed National Cyber Director within the executive branch. With cyber touching so many aspects of our lives (and government), we need someone at the highest level who can wrangle all the federal agencies to move the ball forward.

name: Senator Angus King
tweet: ➡️Congress should create House Permanent Select and Senate Select Committees. Right now, cyber responsibility is diluted across committees, creating a sometimes insurmountable obstacle to progress. We can’t let Congressional bureaucracy impede necessary action.

name: Senator Angus King
tweet: To help enact these layers, we’ve created six pillars of deterrence and more than 75 specific recommendations focused on improving security in both the public and private sectors. Some of our key findings:

name: Senator Angus King
tweet: There is no silver bullet here, but there may be silver buckshot. America needs to pursue layered cyber deterrence by enhancing our defenses at home, reducing benefits for those who would try to exploit our cyber vulnerabilities, and imposing costs for those who cross the line.

name: Senator Angus King
tweet: The CSC brought together bipartisan Members of Congress – including my co-chair @RepGallagher, @SenSasse, and @JimLangevin – top executive branch officials, and industry leaders, drawing from the different perspectives we need to face this pressing challenge head on.

name: Senator Angus King
tweet: To be blunt: we are dangerously insecure in cyberspace, even as we become more reliant on it. Everything from your bank account to your health records to your electricity relies on cyber; but America has serious vulnerabilities that, when exploited, inflict lasting damage.

name: Senator Angus King
tweet: For a year, the @CyberSolarium has worked to review the threats America faces in cyberspace and provide recommendations on how to address them. Today, we’re releasing our report, which we hope will help guide the country in the years ahead. Read:  https://t.co/ViDnPftEuG
